title: The tech interview is broken. Here's how top companies bypass it now
post:
The whiteboard interview is dead. Top companies know it.

Leetcode-style coding challenges measure how well someone performs under artificial pressure solving irrelevant problems. They don't predict job performance. They select for people with time to grind 500 problems.

So what's replacing it?

At top companies, it's work-sample tests. Pay candidates to build something real for a day. See how they think, how they ask questions, how they handle ambiguity.

Other companies use AI-assisted interviews — structured conversations analyzed for communication quality, not just correctness.

Some skip technical interviews entirely and rely on portfolio and references. Revolutionary concept: hire people who have already done the work.

The companies still doing whiteboard interviews are often the ones that can't attract enough candidates to be selective. Once you're Google, you can do things differently.

How does your company interview developers? Thoughts?
